quizQuestion: Who is the creator of the manga series Spy x Family?
explanation: Tatsuya Endo began serializing Spy x Family in Shueisha's digital magazine Shonen Jump+ in March 2019. The story follows a spy, an assassin, and a telepath child all pretending to be a normal family.

quizQuestion: Which two animation studios co-produced the Spy x Family anime that premiered in 2022?
explanation: The Spy x Family anime adaptation that premiered in April 2022 was a rare co-production between Wit Studio and CloverWorks. The two studios split production duties across the cours of the series.

quizQuestion: By 2023, approximately how many million copies of the Spy x Family manga were in circulation?
explanation: Spy x Family surpassed 34 million copies in circulation by 2023, making it one of the fastest-growing manga series of its era. The series' breakout character Anya Forger became a widely beloved internet meme.

quizQuestion: Who created the manga Chainsaw Man, serialized in Weekly Shonen Jump starting in 2018?
explanation: Tatsuki Fujimoto created Chainsaw Man, which ran in Weekly Shonen Jump from 2018 to 2020 for Part 1, then moved to Shueisha's digital platform Jump+ for Part 2 starting in 2022. MAPPA produced the anime adaptation that premiered in October 2022.

quizQuestion: Which animation studio produced the Chainsaw Man anime that premiered in 2022?
explanation: MAPPA produced the Chainsaw Man anime adaptation, which premiered in October 2022 to widespread acclaim. The studio also produces Attack on Titan's final season and Jujutsu Kaisen, making it one of the most prominent anime studios of the early 2020s.

quizQuestion: What is the name of the protagonist in Chainsaw Man who merges with a chainsaw devil?
explanation: Denji is the protagonist of Chainsaw Man, a young man who merges with his pet devil Pochita and gains the power to manifest chainsaws from his body. The series is known for its dark, subversive take on shonen action tropes.

quizQuestion: Solo Leveling, one of the world's most popular manhwa series, originally comes from which country?
explanation: Solo Leveling originated as a South Korean web novel by Chugong before being adapted into a manhwa (Korean comic). Its global popularity helped introduce millions of international readers to manhwa as a format distinct from Japanese manga.

quizQuestion: What is the name of the protagonist in Solo Leveling who starts as the weakest hunter and gains the ability to level up?
explanation: Sung Jinwoo is the protagonist of Solo Leveling, initially ranked as the weakest hunter in the world until he is chosen as a 'Player' by a mysterious system that grants him the unique ability to grow stronger through leveling up. The A-1 Pictures anime adaptation premiered in January 2024.

quizQuestion: Which animation studio produced the Solo Leveling anime that premiered in January 2024?
explanation: A-1 Pictures produced the Solo Leveling anime adaptation, which premiered in January 2024 and became one of the most anticipated anime of the year. A-1 Pictures is also known for producing Sword Art Online, Fairy Tail, and various other major titles.

quizQuestion: Who created the Sword Art Online light novel series that began in 2009?
explanation: Reki Kawahara originally published Sword Art Online as a web novel in 2002 before it was picked up for official publication as a light novel in 2009. The A-1 Pictures anime adaptation that aired in 2012 became a massive hit and helped popularize the isekai and game-world subgenres.

quizQuestion: What is the nickname of the protagonist Kazuto Kirigaya in Sword Art Online?
explanation: Kazuto Kirigaya uses the in-game username Kirito in Sword Art Online. As a solo 'beater' player who becomes one of the most powerful swordsmen in the virtual world, Kirito became one of the most recognizable anime protagonists of the 2010s.

quizQuestion: Which Dragon Ball Z character delivers the iconic 'It's Over 9000!' line while crushing a scouter?
explanation: Vegeta delivers the famous line while scanning Goku's power level in the English dub of Dragon Ball Z, though the original Japanese line was actually 'It's over 8000!' The discrepancy became a notable piece of anime localization trivia and an enduring internet meme.

quizQuestion: After which historical figure is Goku's signature Kamehameha energy attack named?
explanation: The Kamehameha attack is named after King Kamehameha I, the Hawaiian monarch who unified the Hawaiian Islands in the early 19th century. Akira Toriyama named Master Roshi's technique after the king because he liked the sound of the word; the move has since become one of the most iconic energy attacks in all of anime.

quizQuestion: What is the pen name of the artist who originally created One Punch Man as a webcomic in 2009?
explanation: ONE created One Punch Man as a self-published webcomic starting in 2009. The story's premise — a hero so powerful he defeats every enemy in one punch and finds no satisfaction in fighting — became wildly popular, leading manga artist Yusuke Murata to create a high-quality manga remake starting in 2012.

quizQuestion: Which manga artist produced the highly acclaimed redraw of One Punch Man, renowned for its spectacular action artwork?
explanation: Yusuke Murata, previously known for Eyeshield 21, produced the official manga adaptation of One Punch Man starting in 2012 with art so detailed and dynamic that it set a new standard for action manga illustration. Madhouse then produced the Season 1 anime in 2015 to critical acclaim.

quizQuestion: Which studio produced the critically acclaimed Season 1 of One Punch Man in 2015?
explanation: Madhouse produced the first season of the One Punch Man anime in 2015, which became famous for its fluid, high-quality animation particularly during action sequences. The series is a satire of shonen power escalation, with protagonist Saitama able to defeat any enemy with a single punch.

quizQuestion: Who created the Tokyo Ghoul manga series, which ran in Weekly Young Jump from 2011 to 2018?
explanation: Sui Ishida created Tokyo Ghoul, which serialized in Shueisha's Weekly Young Jump magazine from 2011 to 2014, followed by the sequel Tokyo Ghoul:re from 2014 to 2018. The Pierrot anime adaptation introduced the dark urban fantasy story to a global audience.

quizQuestion: What is the name of the half-ghoul protagonist of Tokyo Ghoul who becomes caught between human and ghoul worlds?
explanation: Ken Kaneki is a college student who becomes a half-ghoul after a near-fatal encounter, forcing him to navigate life between human society and the hidden ghoul world. His transformation, including his iconic white hair, became one of anime's most recognizable character design moments.

quizQuestion: Who created the basketball manga Slam Dunk, which ran in Weekly Shonen Jump from 1990 to 1996?
explanation: Takehiko Inoue created Slam Dunk, which ran for 276 chapters in Weekly Shonen Jump and is widely credited with popularizing basketball throughout Japan. The manga has sold over 170 million copies, and Inoue revisited the franchise with the 2022 film The First Slam Dunk.

quizQuestion: Approximately how many million copies of the Slam Dunk manga have been sold worldwide?
explanation: Slam Dunk by Takehiko Inoue has sold over 170 million copies worldwide, making it one of the best-selling manga series of all time. The manga single-handedly sparked a basketball boom in Japan during the 1990s.

quizQuestion: What was the title of the 2022 Slam Dunk theatrical film that earned over $250 million worldwide?
explanation: The First Slam Dunk, released in December 2022 and directed by original manga creator Takehiko Inoue, earned over $256 million worldwide. It became one of the highest-grossing anime films ever and introduced the franchise to a new generation of fans.

quizQuestion: What does the Japanese word 'isekai' (異世界) literally mean in English?
explanation: Isekai literally means 'another world' or 'different world' in Japanese, referring to the genre where a protagonist is transported to a fantasy world. The genre exploded in popularity in the 2010s, with series like Sword Art Online, Re:Zero, KonoSuba, and That Time I Got Reincarnated as a Slime.

quizQuestion: Who created the detective manga Detective Conan (Case Closed), which has been serialized since 1994?
explanation: Gosho Aoyama began serializing Detective Conan in Shogakukan's Weekly Shonen Sunday in January 1994. The manga has run for over 1,100 chapters and remains ongoing, making it one of the longest-running manga series in publication history.

quizQuestion: Approximately how many million copies of Detective Conan (Case Closed) have been sold globally?
explanation: Detective Conan has sold over 270 million copies worldwide, placing it among the best-selling manga series of all time alongside One Piece and Golgo 13. The TMS Entertainment anime adaptation has aired over 1,100 episodes since 1996.

quizQuestion: Which manga creator is responsible for both Yu Yu Hakusho and Hunter x Hunter?
explanation: Yoshihiro Togashi created both Yu Yu Hakusho (1990-1994) and Hunter x Hunter (1998-present), making him one of the most celebrated shonen manga authors. Yu Yu Hakusho ran for 175 chapters in Weekly Shonen Jump and was adapted into a 112-episode anime by Pierrot.

quizQuestion: Which creator, also known for One Punch Man, wrote and drew Mob Psycho 100?
explanation: ONE created Mob Psycho 100 as a webcomic starting in 2012, running it alongside One Punch Man. Bones produced two seasons of the anime (2016 and 2019) plus a third season in 2022, all of which were praised for their innovative animation style depicting psychic powers.

quizQuestion: What is the real name of the psychically overpowered middle school student nicknamed 'Mob' in Mob Psycho 100?
explanation: Shigeo Kageyama, nicknamed Mob because of his plain appearance, is an extremely powerful esper who tries to suppress his emotions to avoid losing control of his psychic powers. His mentor is the fraudulent psychic Arataka Reigen, who forms the heart of the series' comedic and emotional core.

quizQuestion: Who created the Fairy Tail manga, which ran in Weekly Shonen Magazine from 2006 to 2017?
explanation: Hiro Mashima created Fairy Tail, which ran for 545 chapters in Kodansha's Weekly Shonen Magazine from 2006 to 2017. The anime adaptation was produced by A-1 Pictures and later Satelight, totaling over 300 episodes. Mashima is also known for creating Rave Master and Edens Zero.

quizQuestion: In which magazine was the Fairy Tail manga serialized by publisher Kodansha?
explanation: Fairy Tail was serialized in Kodansha's Weekly Shonen Magazine, a rival to Shueisha's Weekly Shonen Jump. While Jump is the more globally famous publication, Magazine has a long history of hit series including initial serializations of Hajime no Ippo, Negima, and Elfen Lied.
